description:
## Component: hx-link (NEW)
**Tier 1 — Universal Foundation | Industry Consensus: 4/6 libraries**

Styled anchor element with inline and standalone variants. Ensures consistent link styling across Drupal-rendered content.

### ADR-001 Classification: Property-Driven (Atomic)
Atomic component. Properties control visual treatment. Default slot accepts link text.

### API Design

**Properties:**
- `href`: string (REQUIRED)
- `target`: _self | _blank | _parent | _top
- `rel`: string (auto-adds noopener noreferrer when target=_blank)
- `variant`: default | subtle | primary | danger
- `inline`: boolean (inline text link vs standalone block link)
- `external`: boolean (shows external link icon)
- `disabled`: boolean

**Slots:**
- Default slot: Link text/content
- `prefix`: Icon before text
- `suffix`: Icon after text (auto-populated when external=true)

**CSS Parts:**
- `link`: The anchor element
- `external-icon`: External link indicator

### Drupal Integration
```twig
{# Inline link in body text #}
<hx-link href="{{ url('entity.node.canonical', {'node': node.id}) }}">
  View full patient record
</hx-link>

{# External link with auto-icon #}
<hx-link href="https://www.cdc.gov/vaccines/" external target="_blank">
  CDC Vaccine Information
</hx-link>
```

Content lives in default slot — Drupal controls link text. Component handles styling, external indicators, and rel attributes.

### Healthcare Use Cases
- Patient record cross-references
- External resource links (CDC, WHO, drug databases)
- Navigation within clinical workflows

### Acceptance Criteria
1. Renders semantic `<a>` element (not button styled as link)
2. Auto-adds rel="noopener noreferrer" when target="_blank"
3. External variant shows icon suffix
4. Inline variant integrates with surrounding text (no block display)
5. Focus ring visible, meets WCAG 2.1 AA contrast
6. Works inside hx-prose Drupal content
title: T1-04: hx-link — Styled Anchor Element

lastReviewFeedback:
coderabbitai: **Actionable comments posted: 4**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against the current code and only fix it if needed.

Inline comments:
In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-link/hx-link.styles.ts`:
- Around line 28-135: The CSS uses hardcoded fallback values instead of
token-only fallbacks; update all occurrences in the hx-link styles (e.g., in the
.link rule: gap, color, text-decoration, text-underline-offset, transition
timing, border-radius; in
.link:hover/.link:visited/.link:focus-visible/.link--disabled and variant
classes like .link--default, .link--primary, .link--danger; and icon sizes like
.link__external-icon width/height) to remove literal values and reference only
design tokens (use --hx-* token fallbacks, e.g. replace "0.25rem", "#1d4ed8",
"150ms ease", "2px", "0.875em", etc. with appropriate --hx- tokens) ensuring
every property uses a token-only fallback pattern.

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-link/hx-link.test.ts`:
- Around line 33-343: Tests currently use unsafe non-null assertions after
shadowQuery(...) (e.g., shadowQuery<HTMLAnchorElement>(el, 'a')!) which violates
strict-TS rules; replace each "!" by capturing the possibly-null value (const
anchor = shadowQuery<...>(...);), assert it exists with
expect(anchor).toBeTruthy() or expect(anchor).not.toBeNull(), then narrow/alias
it for further checks (e.g., const anchorEl = anchor as HTMLAnchorElement)
before accessing attributes or classList; apply this pattern for all shadowQuery
usages in these HelixLink tests (references: shadowQuery, HelixLink, oneEvent
assertions and all tests that currently append "!" to shadowQuery results).

In `@packages/hx-library/src/components/hx-link/hx-link.ts`:
- Around line 101-127: The external-mode logic should always treat the effective
target as _blank when external is true so rel hardening is applied consistently:
in _computeRel replace the effectiveTarget cal
error: PR exceeded 2 review iterations. Escalated to CTO.
